model.label: GLM-5
model.description: Z.AI • New-generation foundation model capable of providing reliable productivity in long-range agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Arizona enters as the superior team ranked #3 in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite two-way profile—7th in offense and 1st-3rd in defense—while Purdue sits #8 with the nation's top offense but a vulnerable defense ranked 36th. The decisive mismatch is Arizona's ability to get stops against Purdue's elite offense, while the Boilermakers' porous defense will struggle to contain Arizona's balanced attack led by Big 12 Player of the Year Jaden Bradley and star freshman Koa Peat. Purdue's four-games-in-four-days Big Ten tournament run raises legitimate fatigue concerns, and their late-season struggles contrast with Arizona's season-long dominance and 11-game winning streak. While Braden Smith and Purdue's frontcourt make them dangerous, Arizona's defensive prowess and overall completeness give them a clear path to the Final Four.

reasoningStep.title: West Regional Final: Arizona vs Purdue
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed team ratings, injury status, momentum, and the critical offense-vs-defense matchup to determine Arizona's advantage.
reasoningStep.evidence: Arizona ranks #3 in both KenPom and Torvik with elite defense (1st-3rd nationally) and top-10 offense, while Purdue ranks #8 with #1 offense but #36 defense. Arizona won Big 12 regular season and tournament titles with Jaden Bradley (Big 12 POY) and Koa Peat healthy after returning from injury. Purdue won four games in four days in Big Ten tournament but had 4 losses in 6 games before that run. The efficiency margin gap is approximately 7 points per 100 possessions favoring Arizona, and Purdue's fatigue from their grueling tournament run is a concern.
